items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.correct: The ability of different classes to respond to the same method call in their own way.
items.misconception: Having multiple classes with the same name.
items.random_wrong: Variables that change type at runtime.
items.why: Students confuse polymorphism with naming. Polymorphism means 'many forms' - a single interface (method signature) behaving differently depending on the object's actual class.

items.q: Why is UDP used for live video streaming despite being unreliable?
items.correct: Lower overhead and latency - retransmitting late packets would harm real-time quality.
items.misconception: Because TCP cannot handle video data.
items.random_wrong: Because UDP is always faster than TCP.
items.why: Students assume reliability is always desirable. For real-time media, a dropped packet is preferable to a delayed one - UDP trades reliability for timeliness, which suits live media.

items.q: Which technique involves breaking a problem into smaller sub-problems?
items.why: Correct answer: Decomposition. “Abstraction” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the purpose of a trace table?
items.correct: To track variable values during execution
items.why: Correct answer: To track variable values during execution. “To compile code” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many distinct shapes are commonly used in basic flowcharts (start/end, process, decision, input/output)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 shapes. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
